blocks.content: After receiving the call, he arrived at the Karma Management Department
and bowed respectfully to Lethe's orders.

blocks.speaker: Boyd
blocks.content: Yes, yes.
Unlike Monoras, Boyd was efficient in his work and had no desire for material wealth.

blocks.speaker: Lethe
blocks.content: When you arrive in Raviette, meet with the chief of staff first. It seems they've been delayed due to an issue... Anyway, after that, go to the duke and deliver the message exactly as I told you.

blocks.speaker: Boyd
blocks.content: Understood.
If it were up to her, Lethe would have gladly entrusted everything to Boyd.

blocks.speaker: Lethe
blocks.content: Ugh, that insufferable pig.
But thinking about the humiliation Monoras would suffer made it impossible for her to harden her heart completely.

blocks.speaker: Lethe
blocks.content: Proceed as planned. Support the chief of staff well, and make sure there are no errors.

blocks.speaker: Boyd
blocks.content: ...Yes.

blocks.speaker: Boyd
blocks.content: Thinking about working alongside Monoras darkened Boyd's outlook, but how could it be worse than what Lethe endured?

blocks.speaker: Boyd
blocks.content: I'll help you, Director.
To Boyd, Lethe was the most rational figure in Hell and the mother of all demons.

blocks.speaker: Boyd
blocks.content: Monoras is the only one who doesn't get it.
As Boyd left with a cold gaze, Lethe sighed and brushed her hair back.

blocks.speaker: Lethe
blocks.content: Phew, now I'll try to get some sleep...

blocks.speaker: Vivian
blocks.content: Director!

blocks.speaker: Vivian
blocks.content: Classification number 4984! Incident at the Wall of Lamentation! That's where Rian is!
Lethe snapped awake, twisting her body around.

blocks.speaker: Lethe
blocks.content: What's happening?
Without needing further explanation, the screen showed an explosion at the Wall of Lamentation.
Yahweh.
Though it hadn't collapsed entirely, thousands of holes were punctured through it, with humans pouring out.

blocks.speaker: Lethe
blocks.content: That bastard's gone insane! If he wanted to leave, he could have done it quietly! Why destroy the warehouse and cause chaos? How much is the damage?

blocks.speaker: Vivian
blocks.content: It seems like a clash with the Black Monks. The damage is significant, but worse than that, there's a system error...

blocks.speaker: Vivian
blocks.content: Vivian pointed at Rian.

blocks.speaker: Lethe
blocks.content: What should we do about this?
Lethe's face gradually turned pale as she stared at the screen.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Just a little further!
Rian, who was climbing vertically along the left side of the wall, finally found an opening and leaped.
BOOOOM!
A powerful explosion echoed as he twisted his body to see the Wall of Lamentation riddled with holes like Swiss cheese.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Rian!

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: See you at the end!
Crossing the Wall of Lamentation was the only way to reach the Energy Management Corporation.
Unfurling his wings, Shirone avoided the Black Monks and surged forward, while Rian scanned ahead.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Gehenna?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: They were targets for purification.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: You, bound by the agony of flames, are not meant to enjoy freedom. By what arrogant trick have you broken your chains and wandered here?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Because I decided so.
Karma, being a consequence of actions, was an immutable code.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Is this possible?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: For the Black Monks, this was as shocking as a dead man returning to life.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: ...Purify him.
The Black Monks surged forward like smoke.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Punish the sinner!
Their black cloaks rippled like liquid before billowing out into smoke, enveloping the space.
—Krrrgh! How...
Black chains shot toward Rian, and a scythe hidden within the cloak gleamed for a brief moment.
Yaaah!
Rian swung his greatsword, unleashing an erratic, rhythmic slash that tore through the advancing darkness.
By perfectly cutting off their unpredictable movements, Rian caused the Black Monks' wariness to skyrocket.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Be careful. That's the pinnacle of a single-strike technique.
It resembled the zeta function.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: This rhythm doesn't even exist in Hell. Even the gods would frown at such chaos.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Beilouk, a sword technique that encapsulated every possible form into a single linear slash.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Approaching him recklessly will get us sliced instantly.
It was a technique of time, not space, born not from talent but sheer effort.
Yaaah!
Slashing in every direction, Rian moved forward without hesitation.
As the Black Monks struggled, human hands suddenly emerged from the ground.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Don't go.
Gripping his ankles, the hands were torn away, but soon more began clutching at him from every direction.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Now! Finish him off!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Those who wished for misfortune recalled Shirone's words as Rian stomped the ground forcefully.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Out of my way!
Wherever Rian ran, hands rose up, swaying like a field of reeds.
Eventually, pieces of human bodies detached from the wall and began climbing up Rian's body.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Aaaah!
As Rian tried to shake them off and run, he suddenly felt intense heat rising from his chest.
WOOOOO!
A scream loud enough to burst his brain echoed around him.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Urghhh!
—Why are you the only one free? We're suffering, so why should you be happy?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Like that... You are...

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Shut up!
—Die with us. Suffer with us.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: If you are free, I cannot endure it.
The chains of Gehenna were ripped away.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: I said shut up!
Like sweet potato vines being pulled, hundreds of human figures were dragged along with the chains.
—You're just like us. You're no different...

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Their voices infiltrated the deepest recesses of Rian's mind, awakening a hidden part of himself.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Ughhhh!
Clenching his teeth, veins bulged on Rian's neck, and blood trickled from his eyes.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: They called him Ogent Rai, right?
When his elder brother succeeded in sword mastery, every acquaintance of their swordsmanship family said:

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Compared to him, Rian... lacks talent.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: No, it's worse than that. He has no understanding of swordsmanship at all.
Returning home after sword training, Rian overheard them murmuring.
—You hated Rai, didn't you? You wanted to kill him. Why was he given talent while you were not?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Rai is just Rai.
The person Rian truly wanted to kill...

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Yaaaah!
Slashing in every direction, Rian moved forward without hesitation.
As the Black Monks struggled, human hands suddenly emerged from the ground.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Don't go.

blocks.speaker: Rian
